## What We'll Cover Today

Two halves: first **how a robot decides what to do**, then **how it acts safely under contact**.

::: {.two-column-balanced style="align-items:start; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;"}
::: {.compact}
**Part I — Learning to act**

1. Motivation: contact-rich manipulation
2. Imitation learning & generative policies
3. Diffusion models → Diffusion Policy
4. Making inference real-time
:::

::: {.compact}
**Part II — Acting compliantly**

5. Compliant control & variable admittance
6. Case study: a variable-admittance diffusion policy on a real arm
7. Research directions & discussion
:::
:::

::: {.notes}
The lecture is organized as a two-part story, and this map shows where each block fits.

Part I answers "how do we generate robot actions?"; Part II asks "how do those actions behave under contact, and how do we keep them safe?". The case study at the end ties both halves together on real hardware.

Diffusion Policy should not be treated as a complete control solution. It still needs a real robot controller underneath. This map is enough for now; the technical material starts next.
:::
